summary: Comprehensive frontend testing of the new Provinces page feature. All 10 test cases passed successfully. The page displays 60 educational provinces grouped under 26 administrative provinces with 3-level drill-down navigation.

issues: 'Profil incomplet' notification appears on page load - minor UX issue but not blocking

passed_tests: Provinces page loads with 3 stat cards (26 admin provinces, 60 educational provinces, 567 sub-divisions)
Introductory text about the ministry's educational provinces is displayed
PROVED, IPP, DIPROCOPE committee explanation cards are shown with descriptions
Search bar filters provinces by name (tested with 'Kinshasa' - filtered to 1 result)
26 administrative province cards are rendered in a grid with P.E. and S.D. counts
Clicking Kinshasa province card shows its 5 educational provinces (Lukunga, Funa, Mont-Amba, Tshangu, Plateau)
Educational province cards show contacts (PROVED, IPP, DIPROCOPE names)
Clicking Lukunga educational province shows the sub-divisions table with 13 entries
Sub-divisions table has columns: #, NOM, LIEU D'IMPLANTATION
Back navigation works at all levels (edu province → admin province → all provinces)
context_for_next_testing_agent: Provinces page feature is complete and working. Static data from provincesEducationnelles.js contains all 60 educational provinces with contacts and sub-divisions. Navigation flow: Admin provinces grid → Educational provinces cards → Sub-divisions table. All data-testid attributes are properly implemented for testing.
